item.title: 这周你赢了什么？ | What was your win this week?
item.description:
迈克尔·萨灵顿

发表于嘿伙计！ 希望每个人都度过了一个美好的星期五，希望你们都度过了美好的周末！

回顾过去的一周，你为完成的事情感到自豪吗？

所有的胜利都很重要——无论大小🎉

开始一个新项目

  • 修复一个棘手的错误
  • 雕刻南瓜🎃

    • 穿上你的周末脸！ 创建一个帐户，加入数十万 DEV 成员的旅程。

    亚历山大·利伯拉托—— 一旦暂停，michaeltharrington 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

    一旦解除暂停，michaeltharrington 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

    一旦未发表，michaeltharrington 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

    如果 michaeltharrington 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

    感谢您保护 DEV 社区👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 michaeltharrington 的方法：

    举报其他不当行为

    取消标记 michaeltharrington 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



    原文:

    Michael Tharrington

    Posted on Oct 21

    Hey folks! 👋

    Hope everybody is having a fantastic Friday and that you all have wonderful weekends!

    Looking back on this past week, what was something you were proud of accomplishing?

    All wins count — big or small 🎉

    Examples of 'wins' include:

    • Starting a new project
    • Fixing a tricky bug
    • Carving a pumpkin 🎃

    Put your weekend face on! 😎

     🗒 Share a tutorial🤔 Reflect on your coding journey❓ Ask a question 

    Create an account to join hundreds of thousands of DEV members on their journey.

    Alexandre Liberato - Jul 28

    Once suspended, michaeltharrington will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

    Once unsuspended, michaeltharrington will be able to comment and publish posts again.

item.title: 关注周五：NoSQL 版（2022 年 10 月 21 日） | Follow Friday: NoSQL Edition (21 October 2022)
item.description:
朋友们五一快乐！ 查看评论并关注新朋友！

顾名思义，NoSQL 数据库是非 SQL（或不仅仅是 SQL），这意味着它们使用关系表以外的格式存储和检索数据。

所以，开发者！ 你最喜欢的关于 NoSQL 数据库的作者是谁？

格式化提示：要使用关注按钮填充卡片，请使用流动标签语法{% embed https://... %}并插入您最喜欢的作者个人资料的 URL。

加入#FollowFriday 乐趣，@提及您最喜欢的作者以关注 NoSQL 的主题（并让我们知道您为什么喜欢他们的帖子！）⤵️

一旦暂停，devteam 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，devteam 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，devteam 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 devteam 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 devteam 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 devteam 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Happy Friday, friends! 🎉 Follow Friday is your weekly opportunity to shout out fellow DEV Community members doing awesome work. Check out the comments and follow someone new!

As the name suggests, NoSQL databases are non-SQL (or not-only-SQL), meaning that they store and retrieve data using a format other than relational tables.

Some popular examples of NoSQL databases are Redis, MongoDB, Azure's Cosmos DB, Amazon's DynamoDB, Elasticsearch, Couchbase — and the list goes on!

So, devs! Who are your favorite authors writing about NoSQL databases?

Formatting tip: to populate a card with a follow button, use the liquid tag syntax {% embed https://... %} and insert the URL of your favorite author's profile.

Join in on the #FollowFriday fun by @mentioning your favorite author to follow on the topic of NoSQL (and letting us know why you love their posts!) ⤵️

item.title: 开始使用 AWS Config 规则和修复 | Get Started with AWS Config Rules and Remediation
item.description:
这包括资源如何相互关联以及资源过去是如何组织的，因此您可以看到它们的配置和关系如何随时间变化。

特征

  • 资源管理

  • 规则和一致性包

  • 聚合器

  • 高级查询

图片来源

  • 当您启用 AWS Config 时，它首先会识别您账户中支持的 AWS 资源，并为每个资源创建一个配置项。
  • 默认情况下，AWS Config 为区域中的每个受支持资源创建一个配置项。
  • 如果您不希望 AWS Config 为所有受支持的资源创建配置项，您可以指定要跟踪的资源类型。

该服务使用相同的 API 调用来检索所有相关资源的配置详细信息。

例如，

如果您从 VPC 安全组中删除出口规则，AWS Config 会对该安全组进行 API 描述调用。

安全组（资源）和每个实例（相关资源）的更新配置被记录为配置项，并交付到配置流程中的 Amazon Simple Storage Service 文件夹。

如果您使用 AWS Config 规则，AWS Config 会持续评估您的 AWS 资源配置以获得所需的设置。 根据规则，AWS Config 会定期评估资源或响应配置更改。

每个规则都与一个包含规则评估逻辑的AWS Lambda 函数相关联。 当 AWS Config 评估资源时，它会调用规则的 AWS Lambda 函数。 此函数返回评估资源的匹配状态。

如果资源违反规则条件，AWS Config 会将资源和规则标记为不合规。 当资源的合规状态发生变化时，AWS Config 会向 Amazon SNS 主题发送通知。

请观看以下视频以了解有关 AWS Config 的更多信息。

合规管理

设想

使用 AWS Config 确定哪些弹性网络接口或 ENI 未附加到您环境中的资源，然后使用 System Manager 恢复标记为不合规的资源。

  • 可以为这些规则提供捆绑的合规性补救措施，这些措施可以在个人账户或组织级别实施。
  • 偏离规则的初始配置或配置更改会自动触发 Amazon Simple Notification Service (SNS) 通知和 Amazon CloudWatch 事件，以便您随时了解情况。

在开始使用配置之前，您必须启用配置录制并配置交付通道。

请观看以下视频以开始使用 AWS Config 规则和补救措施。

结论

在本文中，您创建了许多未附加的弹性 IP 地址并添加了 AWS 配置。 然后，您实施了一个规则来检查未附加的 EIP，并使用 Systems Manager Automation 运行手册来修复不合规的资源。

感谢您阅读我的文章直到最后。 我希望你今天意识到一些独特的事情。 如果您喜欢这篇文章，请与您的朋友分享，如果您有建议或想法与我分享，请在评论框中写下。

关注我，分享你的想法，
领英
推特

上述博客是在 Devtron 举办的“ Devtron Hacktoberfest 2022”下提交的。 查看他们的 Github 存储库，如果您喜欢，请给它一个星 ⭐️。 在LinkedIn和Twitter 上关注 Devtron



原文:

AWS Config provides a detailed view of the configuration of AWS resources in your AWS account. This includes how resources are related to each other and how resources have been organized in the past, so you can see how their configurations and relationships change over time.

Features

  • Resource management

  • Rules and conformance packs

  • Aggregators

  • Advanced queries

Image Source

  • When you enable AWS Config, it first identifies the supported AWS resources in your account and creates a configuration item for each resource.
  • AWS Config also creates a configuration item when the resource configuration changes and keeps a historical record of the resource configuration item after the configuration author runs.
  • By default, AWS Config creates a configuration item for each supported resource in a region.
  • If you don't want AWS Config to create configuration items for all supported resources, you can specify the types of resources you want to track.

AWS Config tracks all resource changes by calling the Describe or List API calls for each resource in your account. The service uses the same API call to retrieve configuration details for all related resources.

For example,

If you delete an egress rule from a VPC security group, AWS Config makes an API describe call on the security group.

AWS Config then makes an API call to describe all instances associated with the security group. Updated configurations for security groups (resources) and each instance (related resources) are recorded as configuration items and delivered to the Amazon Simple Storage Service folder in the configuration flow.

AWS Config also tracks configuration changes not implemented in the API. AWS Config periodically checks the resource configuration and creates configuration items for changed configurations.

AWS Config rules

If you use AWS Config rules, AWS Config continuously evaluates the configuration of your AWS resources for the desired settings. Depending on the rule, AWS Config evaluates resources periodically or in response to configuration changes.

Each rule is associated with an AWS Lambda function that contains the rule's evaluation logic. When AWS Config evaluates a resource, it invokes the rule's AWS Lambda function. This function returns the match status of the evaluated resource.

If a resource violates the conditions of a rule, AWS Config marks the resource and the rule as non-compliant. When the compliance status of resource changes, AWS Config sends a notification to an Amazon SNS topic.

Kindly watch the below video to know more about AWS Config.

Compliance management

AWS Config is a service that allows you to view, monitor, and evaluate the configuration of your AWS resources. Config continuously monitors and logs AWS source configurations, which can automate the evaluation of logged configurations against desired configurations.

Scenario

Use AWS Config to determine which elastic network interfaces or ENIs are not attached to resources in your environment, and then use System Manager to restore resources marked as non-compliant.

  • AWS Config allows you to define rules for evaluating the provisioning and configuration of AWS resources. These rules can be provided with bundled compliance remediation actions that can be implemented at the individual account or organization level.
  • Initial configuration or configuration changes that deviate from the rules automatically trigger Amazon Simple Notification Service (SNS) notifications and Amazon CloudWatch events so you can stay informed.

Before you can start using the configuration, you must enable the configuration recording and configure a delivery channel.

Kindly watch the below video to get started with AWS Config rules and remediation.

Conclusion

In this article, you created a number of unattached elastic IP addresses and added an AWS configuration. You then implemented a rule to check for unattached EIPs and used Systems Manager Automation runbooks to fix the non-compliant resources.

Gratitude for perusing my article till the end. I hope you realized something unique today. If you enjoyed this article then please share it with your buddies and if you have suggestions or thoughts to share with me then please write in the comment box.

Follow me and share your thoughts,
GitHub
LinkedIn
Twitter

The above blog is submitted under 'Devtron Hacktoberfest 2022' conducted by Devtron. Check out their Github repo and give it a star ⭐️ if you like it. Follow Devtron on LinkedIn, Twitter
item.author: makendrang

item.title: 本周讨论和评论 - v21 | Discussion and Comment of the Week - v21
item.description:
这份每周综述重点介绍了我们认为本周最深思熟虑和/或最有趣的讨论。 我们还将在每一期中突出一个特别酷的评论。 因此，我们希望鼓励人们参与讨论并奖励那些在社区中发起或参与对话的人。 毕竟，社区是通过人们在其中互动而成为可能的。

本周讨论

对于本周的本周讨论，我想强调两个正在进行的不同有趣的讨论......

可以平衡吗？

这两个讨论都值得关注（如果您还没有留下评论，也许可以发表评论😉）。

我绝对可以理解@xcoderx想要休假以消除倦怠的愿望。 并且有很多有用的回应，当然，正如你所期望的那样，指导相互矛盾……毕竟，这里没有正确的答案，但听听其他人的经验还是很有帮助的。

我们中的很多人都知道一次承担一堆项目并被拉入一百万个不同方向的混乱感觉。 虽然我不是开发人员，但我个人经常用我自己的待办事项列表来面对这场斗争。 与我们之前强调的讨论类似，人们跳进了这个讨论，并提出了一些非常有用的建议，这些建议围绕着自己的节奏、消除工作环境中的干扰以及为项目定义一个终点的重要性。 所有伟大的建议！ 本周评论

至于我们本周的评论， @jmfayard对@devsyedmohsin的“什么是成功”的深思熟虑的回应占据了上风！

注意到成功的定义通常取决于您所处的文化，Jean-Michel 的评论有助于拉开帷幕并揭示在定义成功中发挥作用的环境因素。 听到 Max Weber 的观点以及美国以他们的方式定义成功的一些历史/社会学原因，这很有趣。 此外，相比之下，看到日本概念 Ikigai 的维恩图可视化，非常有趣和相关。 意识到在定义成功方面发挥作用的文化压力使我们更容易看到这些压力如何影响我们自己对成功的定义； 在某些情况下，我们可能会发现我们的定义与我们所处的文化非常吻合，或者我们可能会意识到社会所规定的成功与我们自己的成就感和价值感相去甚远。

你的选择是什么？

这个社区里有很多很棒的讨论和评论。 这些只是我们选择强调的几个。 我敦促大家在下面的评论中分享您最喜欢的评论和/或对过去一周的讨论。 如果你愿意，请给作者一个@mention——它可能会让他们感觉良好。 💚 💚



原文:

This weekly roundup highlights what we believe to be the most thoughtful and/or interesting discussion of the week. We're also be highlighting one particularly cool comment in each installment. 🙌

The DEV Community is particularly special because of the kind and thoughtful discussions happening between community members. As such, we want to encourage folks to participate in discussions and reward those who are initiating or taking part in conversations across the community. After all, a community is made possible by the people interacting inside it.

Discussion of the Week

For this week's discussion of the week, I'd like to highlight 2 different interesting discussions that are ongoing...

@xcoderx's Will a break from job affect my career?:

... and @viktoriabors' Work - Life - Side Project triangle. Can be balanced ?

Both of these discussions are worthy of attention (and perhaps a comment if you haven't already left one 😉).

I could definitely relate to @xcoderx's desire to take a sabbatical in an effort to stamp out burnout. And there were so many helpful responses though of course with contradicting guidance as you'd expect... after all, there is no right answer here, but it's helpful to hear from other's experiences nonetheless.

@viktoriabors' post is also really relatable! Lots of us know the chaotic feeling of taking on a bunch of projects at once and getting pulled into a million different directions. While I'm not a dev, I personally face this struggle quite often with my own list of todos. Also similar to the previous discussion we highlighted, folks hopped into this discussion with some really helpful pointers around the importance of pacing yourself, eliminating distractions in your work environment, and defining a finishing point for your projects. All great advice! 🙌

Comment of the Week

As for our comment of the week, @jmfayard's thoughtful response to @devsyedmohsin's What is Success takes the cake!

Noting that success if often defined differently depending on the culture you're in, Jean-Michel's comment helps pull back the curtain and reveal the environmental factors that play a role in defining success. It was interesting hearing Max Weber's take and some of the historical/sociological reasons for the United States defining success the way they have. Also, in contrast, seeing the Venn diagram visualization of the Japanese concept Ikigai, was super interesting and relatable. Being aware of the cultural pressures that play a role in defining success allows us to more easily see how these pressures have affected our own definition of success; in some instances, we may find that our definitions line up well with the culture we're in, or perhaps we may realize that what society has dictated as success is a far cry from our own feelings of accomplishment and sense of worth.

What are your picks?

There's loads of great discussions and comments floating about in this community. These are just a few we chose to highlight. 🙂

I urge you all to share your favorite comment and/or discussion of the past week below in the comments. And if you're up for it, give the author an @mention — it'll probably make 'em feel good. 💚
item.author: michaeltharrington

item.title: 如何在十分钟内在 AWS Amplify 上使用 Hugo 部署网站 | How to Deploy Website Using Hugo on AWS Amplify in Ten Minutes
item.description:
在这篇文章中，您将学习如何使用Hugo （用于内容管理的 AWS Cloud9 的集成开发环境 (IDE)）、用于源代码管理的AWS CodeCommit和用于服务基于源的网站的AWS Amplify自动设置和部署网站部署过程。

配置 AWS Cloud9 IDE

首先预置 AWS Cloud9 IDE。

您必须在您的 AWS 账户的 Amazon Virtual Private Cloud 的公有子网中预置 AWS Cloud9 环境。

这可以通过以下步骤完成：

  • 使用具有管理员权限的凭证登录您的 AWS 账户。 如果您没有 AWS 账户，您可以创建一个。

  • 打开 Cloud9 控制台并单击Create Environment 。

  • 使用 AWS Cloud9 控制台中的向导创建新的 AWS Cloud9 环境。

  • 输入桌面名称和附加说明。

  • 选择下一步。

  • 在 Environment Settings 部分的 Environment type 下，选择Create a new environment EC2 instance (direct access) 。

  • 对于 Instance Type，选择所需的实例类型。

  • 对于网络，在网络设置 (VPC) 下，选择将托管您的 AWS Cloud9 实例的 VPC。

  • 选择此 VPC 的公有子网进行部署。

  • 保持所有其他设置不变，然后选择Next Step 。

  • 查看选择并选择Create Environment 。 设置环境需要几分钟时间。

  • 当您的环境准备就绪时，您可以从浏览器访问 AWS Cloud9 IDE。

配置源代码存储库以跟踪内容更改

使用 AWS CodeCommit，这是一种托管基于 Git 的安全存储库的完全托管的资源管理服务。

  • 在新浏览器中打开 CodeCommit 控制台并创建一个新存储库。

  • 在存储库名称中，输入amplify-website 。

  • 输入适当的描述。

  • 选择创建。

创建存储库需要几分钟时间。

设置和部署示例网站

  • 在 AWS Cloud9 IDE 中，返回浏览器并将光标放在 IDE 的下部终端窗口中。

  • 输入以下代码以下载并解压缩保存为 .zip 文件的现有示例网站。

 cd ~/environment aws s3 cp s3://ee-assets-prod-us-east-1/modules/3c5ba9cb6ff44465b96993d210f67147/v1/example-website.zip ~/environment/example-website.zip unzip example-website.zip rm example-website.zip

以下屏幕截图显示了结果。

  • 然后运行命令以创建一个目录来托管并通过从示例网站复制文件来启动网站。
 mkdir ~/environment/amplify-website/ cd ~/environment/amplify-website/
  • 接下来，在您的 AWS Cloud9 实例上创建一个名为 local master 的新默认分支。 然后从示例网站复制文件。
 git init git remote add origin codecommit::us-east-1://amplify-website git remote -v git checkout -b main

  • 在本地添加和提交会将所有更改提交到远程 Amazon Codecommit 存储库。
 cp -rp ~/environment/example-website/* ~/environment/amplify-website/ git add * git commit -am "first commit" git push -u origin main
  • 在 Amplify 控制台中，选择Get Started 。

  • 选择Amplify Hosting并单击开始。

  • 在 Amplify 控制台入门页面上，选择AWS CodeCommit作为您的源代码存储库。

  • 选择继续。

  • 在 Add Repository Branch 页面上，为最近更新的存储库选择存储库。

  • 对于分支，选择主。

  • 选择下一步。

  • 在审核页面上，选择Save and Deploy 。

实施完成后，您可以访问我们的网站查看示例内容。

以下屏幕截图显示了一个示例网站。

对网站进行更改

您现在可以更新主页上的文本字符串并提交和发布此更改。

  • 在 AWS Cloud9 IDE 中，返回浏览器并将光标放在 IDE 的下部终端窗口中。

  • 在导航窗格中，选择~/environment/amplify-website/workshop/content/_index.en.md 。

该文件的内容将在顶部面板的新选项卡中打开。

  • 将LARGE TITLE更改为Makedran Docs 。

  • 通过从“文件”菜单中选择“保存”来保存对_index.en.md的更改。

  • 在 AWS Cloud9 中，在下方终端窗口中运行以下命令以提交更改。
 git add *; git commit -am "homepage update"; git push origin main
  • 返回到 Amplify 控制台以查看如何自动检测更改。

本次更新完成后，访问网站网址，查看主页上的 LARGE TITLE 是否发生了变化。

您可以重复此过程以对您的网站进行基于源的自动更改。

添加自定义域

通过将自定义域添加到您的 Amplify 设置，您的客户可以轻松访问您的内容。 您可以通过Amazon Route 53注册新域，或者如果您在 AWS 之外注册了域，则可以与 Route 53 和 Amplify 集成。

对于我们的使用，域名www.makendran.study是由第三方注册商注册的域名。 首先在 Route 53 上设置一个公共区域。

  • 在 Route 53 控制台中，选择托管区域。

  • 选择创建托管区域。

  • 输入makendran.study作为域名。

  • 输入适当的描述。

  • 在类型下，选择公共托管区域。

  • 选择创建托管区域。

  • 保存响应您域的客户端 DNS 查找的名称服务器的地址。

  • 在单独的浏览器中，访问 DNS 注册器控制台。

  • 在远程域名注册控制台中配置自定义 DNS 名称服务器设置。 此配置指向 Route 53 目标名称服务器作为您的自定义域的权威 DNS。 为此，此更改通知可能需要长达 48 小时。

  • 使用https://who.is验证您的 AWS 名称服务器是否为您的自定义 Internet 客户端域正确列出。

您现在可以在 Amplify 中设置自定义域。

在 Amplify 控制台的App Settings下，选择Domain Management 。

  • 选择添加域。

  • 为您的域输入自定义域名 ( makendran.study )。

  • 对于子域，设置 www 并选择排除自定义域根。

一段时间后，您将进入 SSL 配置并看到正在进行的域验证。

验证所有权后，您的域将部署到由 Amplify 管理的 Amazon CloudFront 分配，并且域激活完成。

客户现在可以使用自定义域名www.makendran.study访问该网站。

为开发设置子域

为了与 CodeCommit 的开发代码分支保持一致，Amplify 创建了一个开发网站，以便您可以在更改投入生产之前对其进行测试。

  • 转到 AWS Cloud9 IDE 并键入以下命令以使用终端创建开发分支。
 git checkout -b development git branch git remote -v
  • 修改内容以使用主分支中的当前内容将更改推送到 CodeCommit。
 git add *; git commit -am "first development commit"; git push -u origin development
  • 打开并编辑文件~/environment/amplify-website/workshop/content/_index.en.md并将网站的更新路径更改为其他内容。

  • 使用以下代码捕获并推送更改。
 git add *; git commit -am "second development commit"; git push -u origin development

  • 返回到放大网站应用程序。

  • 选择连接分支。

  • 对于 Branch，选择您创建的开发分支。

  • 选择 Next 并单击Save and Deploy 。

在 Amplify 控制台中，您可以查看与开发代码树相对应的网站实例。

  • 在 Amplify 中，访问域管理菜单项以添加自定义子域。

  • 编辑域并添加具有所需名称的子域条目。

  • 通过提交的代​​码和内容更改提交到开发分支。

  • 选择更新。

控制对开发的访问

新内容托管在开发站点上，因此您可以限制访问。

  1. 在 Amplify 控制台中，选择Applications 。

  2. 选择访问控制。

  1. 在访问控制设置下，选择所需的设置。 您可以选择限制全局或每个站点的访问。

打扫干净

  1. 选择您在 Amplify 控制台中创建的应用程序，然后从操作下拉菜单中选择删除应用程序。

  2. 在 CodeCommit 仪表板中，选择您创建的存储库并选择Delete 。

  3. 选择您在 AWS Cloud9 控制面​​板中创建的 IDE，然后选择Delete 。

请观看下面的视频以查看完整的演示。

结论

参考文章



原文:

In this post, you'll learn how to set up and deploy a website using Hugo (AWS Cloud9's Integrated Development Environment (IDE) for content management), AWS CodeCommit for source code management, and AWS Amplify for serving source-based websites, automated deployment process.

Configuring the AWS Cloud9 IDE

Start by provisioning the AWS Cloud9 IDE.

The AWS Cloud9 environment is based on Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud. You must provision an AWS Cloud9 environment in a public subnet in an Amazon Virtual Private Cloud in your AWS account.

This can be done with the following steps:

  • Sign in to your AWS account with credentials that have administrator privileges. If you don't have an AWS account, you can create one.

  • Open the Cloud9 console and Click Create Environment.

  • Use the wizard in the AWS Cloud9 console to create a new AWS Cloud9 environment.

  • Enter a name for your desktop and an additional description.

  • Choose Next Step.

  • In the Environment Settings section, under Environment type, choose Create a new environment EC2 instance (direct access).

  • For Instance Type, choose the desired instance type.

  • For Network, under Network Settings (VPC), choose the VPC that will host your AWS Cloud9 instance.

  • Choose the public subnet of this VPC to deploy.

  • Leave all other settings unchanged and select Next Step.

  • Review the selections and choose Create Environment. It takes a few minutes to set up the environment.

  • When your environment is ready, you can access the AWS Cloud9 IDE from your browser.

Configure the source code repository to track content changes

Use AWS CodeCommit, a fully managed resource management service that hosts secure Git-based repositories.

  • Open the CodeCommit console in a new browser and create a new repository.

  • In Repository Name, enter amplify-website.

  • Enter an appropriate description.

  • Choose Create.

It takes a few minutes to create the repository.

Set up and deploy a sample website

  • In the AWS Cloud9 IDE, return to your browser and place your cursor in the lower terminal window of the IDE.

  • Enter the following code to download and unzip the existing example website saved as a .zip file.

cd ~/environment
aws s3 cp s3://ee-assets-prod-us-east-1/modules/3c5ba9cb6ff44465b96993d210f67147/v1/example-website.zip ~/environment/example-website.zip
unzip example-website.zip
rm example-website.zip

The following screenshot shows the result.

  • Then run the command to create a directory to host and start the website by copying the files from the example website. 
mkdir ~/environment/amplify-website/
cd ~/environment/amplify-website/
  • Next, create a new default branch on your AWS Cloud9 instance called local master. Then copy the files from the example website. 
git init
git remote add origin codecommit::us-east-1://amplify-website
git remote -v
git checkout -b main

  • Adding and committing locally commits all changes to the remote Amazon Codecommit repository. 
cp -rp ~/environment/example-website/* ~/environment/amplify-website/
git add *
git commit -am "first commit"
git push -u origin main
  • In the Amplify console, Choose Get Started.

  • Choose Amplify Hosting and Click Get Started.

  • On the Getting Started with Amplify Console page, choose AWS CodeCommit as your source code repository.

  • Select Continue.

  • On the Add Repository Branch page, select the repository for the recently updated repository.

  • For Branch, select Main.

  • Choose Next.

  • On the review page, choose Save and Deploy.

Amplify builds and deploys your Amplify site in minutes and shows you the progress.

After the implementation is complete, you can visit our website to see sample content.

The following screenshot shows an example website.

Performing Changes to the website

You can now update the text string on the home page and commit and publish this change.

  • In the AWS Cloud9 IDE, return to your browser and place your cursor in the lower terminal window of the IDE.

  • In the navigation pane, choose ~/environment/amplify-website/workshop/content/_index.en.md.

The contents of the file will open in a new tab in the top panel.

  • Change the LARGE TITLE to Makendran Docs.

  • Save the changes to _index.en.md by choosing Save from the File menu.

  • In AWS Cloud9, run the following command in the lower terminal window to commit the changes. 
git add *; git commit -am "homepage update"; git push origin main
  • Return to the Amplify console to see how automatically detects changes.

Amplify walks you through the steps to make changes to your website.

  • After this update is complete, visit the website URL to see if the LARGE TITLE on the home page has changed.

You can repeat this process to make automatic, source-based changes to your website.

Add a custom domain

By adding custom domains to your Amplify setup, your customers can easily access your content. You can register new domains through Amazon Route 53 , or if you have domains registered outside of AWS, you can integrate with Route 53 and Amplify.

For our use, the domain www.makendran.study is a domain name registered by a third party registrar. Route 53 allows you to manage DNS configuration for domains registered outside of AWS.

Start by setting up a public zone on Route 53.

  • In the Route 53 console, choose hosted zone.

  • Choose Create Hosted Zone.

  • Enter makendran.study as the domain name.

  • Enter an appropriate description.

  • Under Type, select Public Hosted zone.

  • Choose Create Hosted Zone.

  • Save the addresses of name servers that respond to clients DNS lookups for your domain.

  • In a separate browser, access the DNS registrar console.

  • Configure custom DNS name server settings in the remote domain name registration console. This configuration points to the Route 53 destination name servers as the authoritative DNS for your custom domain. For this purpose, this change notice may take up to 48 hours.

  • Use https://who.is to verify that your AWS nameservers are listed correctly for your custom Internet client domain.

You can now set up custom domains in Amplify.

Amplify allows you to configure DNS and configure SSL for any custom domain you want.

  • In the Amplify console, under App Settings , select Domain Management.

  • Choose Add domain.

  • Enter a custom domain name for your domain (makendran.study).

  • For subdomain, set www and choose to exclude custom domain root.

Amplify starts the SSL certificate creation process. After a while, you'll be taken to your SSL configuration and see a domain verification in progress.

Amplify verifies domain ownership by creating an example CNAME record in the host zone file. Once ownership is verified, your domain is deployed to Amazon CloudFront distributions managed by Amplify and domain activation is complete.

Customers can now access the website with a custom domain name www.makendran.study.

Setting up a subdomain for development

To align with CodeCommit's development code branch, Amplify creates a development website so you can test changes before they go into production.

  • Go to the AWS Cloud9 IDE and type the following command to create a development branch using the terminal. 
git checkout -b development
git branch
git remote -v
  • Modify a content to push the changes to CodeCommit using the current content from the master branch. 
git add *; git commit -am "first development commit";
git push -u origin development
  • Open and edit the file ~/environment/amplify-website/workshop/content/_index.en.md and change the update path for the website to something else.

  • Capture and push the changes with the following code. 
git add *; git commit -am "second development commit"; git push -u origin development

  • Go back to the amplify-website app.

  • Select Connect Branch.

  • For Branch, select the development branch you created.

  • Choose Next and Click Save and Deploy.

Amplify will create a second website based on the content of the development branch. In the Amplify console, you can view instances of your website that correspond to the development code tree.

  • In Amplify, access the Domain Management menu item to add a custom subdomain.

  • Edit the domain and add a subdomain entry with the desired name.

  • Commit to the development branch with committed code and content changes.

  • Choose Update.

Control access to development

New content is hosted on the development site, so you can restrict access.

  1. In the Amplify console, select Applications.

  2. Select Access Control.

  1. Under Access control settings, select the desired settings. You have the option to restrict access globally or per site.

Cleaning Up

  1. Select the application you created in the Amplify console and Select Delete App from the Actions drop-down menu.

  2. In the CodeCommit dashboard, choose the repository you created and Choose Delete.

  3. Select the IDE you created in the AWS Cloud9 dashboard and Choose Delete.

Kindly watch the below video to see the full demo.

Conclusion

Hugo is a powerful tool for quickly delivering content in a variety of formats, including image portfolios, online resume presentations, blogs, and technical articles. Amplify Console offers a convenient, easy-to-use static web hosting service that dramatically speeds up the delivery of static content. Hugo and Amplify Console work together to quickly deploy a website in minutes with features like easy-to-use URLs, code-level experiences, and encryption (SSL).

Reference Article

item.title: 欢迎来到 SNGVFX | WELCOME TO SNGVFX
item.description:
一旦暂停，sngvfx 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，sngvfx 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，sngvfx 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 sngvfx 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 sngvfx 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 sngvfx 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Once suspended, sngvfx will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, sngvfx will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by sngvfx will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If sngvfx is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Thanks for keeping DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻 safe. Here is what you can do to flag sngvfx:

sngvfx consistently posts content that violates DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging sngvfx will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: sngvfx

item.title: 嵌入式系统周刊 #123 | Embedded Systems Weekly #123
item.description:
这是 RTOS 公告的一周。 一方面，我们将久负盛名的旧 NuttX 更新到版本 11。另一方面，我们拥有闪亮的新 KataOS，其具有令人印象深刻的规格和目标，由……Google 带给我们。 我真的不想过分强调谷歌在长期维护任何东西方面的​​声誉（想想问题 114中涉及的 IoT Core 服务的关闭），但目前谁能押注谷歌产品？

快乐阅读！

文章

如果您需要在支持 UEFI 的系统上设置安全启动，本文将帮助您立即开始。

自动驾驶2.0
这篇文章由一位从事自动驾驶卡车工作的工程师撰写，很好地解释了在开阔道路上实现车辆完全自主的最有希望的方法。

本课程直接进入学习ARM汇编的最佳课程之首。 它的结构非常好，由于良好的插图或代码示例，我发现不同的部分很清楚。 要加入书签。

在本次演讲中，Hideki Takase 展示了他的项目mros2 。 对于不熟悉机器人领域的人来说，ROS2 是一个专门用于帮助创建机器人的操作系统。

电子邮件 + git = <3
所有对 Linux 内核开发感兴趣的人都知道它是由电子邮件驱动的。 但我们中的许多人不知道它实际上是如何工作的。 有了这个资源，您将准备好做出贡献，而不会被 Linus 告知。

新闻/商业

宣布 KataOS 和 Sparrow
它基于 seL4，几乎完全使用 Rust 并具有安全系统的参考实现（在 RISC-V 架构上使用 OpenTitan 构建）。 听起来不错，但就像我在介绍中已经写的那样：我们可以安全地投资 Google 产品吗？

我希望 NuttX 社区不会被我狭隘的观点所冒犯。

工具/库

戈博特
对于 C 开发人员来说，Go 语言可能是语法上最接近的语言。 请注意，这是一种垃圾收集语言。

工作

作为一个全球品牌，他们的放大器和处理器被用于一些世界上最负盛名的场馆和活动。

杂项

对 Cat 喂料器进行逆向工程以提高生产力
我不太相信通过对自己使用狗训练技术来成为一个更好的开发人员，但至少它很有趣！



原文:

It's a week of RTOS announcements. On the one hand, we have the old long-established NuttX updated to version 11. On the other hand, we have the shiny new KataOS with impressive specifications and goals brought to us by... Google. I really don't want to overemphasize Google's reputation on its capability to maintain anything long term (think about the shutting down of IoT Core service covered in issue 114) but who is presently able to bet on a Google product?

Happy reading!

Articles

Enable UEFI secure boot on Embedded system
This article will get you started in no time if you need to set up a secure boot on your system supporting UEFI.

Self-driving 2.0
Written by an engineer working on self-driving trucks, the article is doing a good job at explaining the most promising approach to achieving full autonomy of vehicles on open roads.

ARM Assembly By Example
This course enters directly to the top of the best courses to learn ARM assembly. It is very well-structured, and I find the different parts clear thanks to the good illustrations or the code examples. To be bookmarked.

mROS 2: yet another runtime environment onto embedded devices
In this presentation, Hideki Takase presents his project mros2. mros2 is an alternative to microROS to run ROS2 on embedded microcontrollers. For people not familiar with the robotics field, ROS2 is a specialised operating system designed to help to create robots.

email + git = <3
All the people who are interested in Linux kernel development know that it is driven by emails. But many of us don't know how it actually works. With this ressource you'll be ready to contribute without being told off by Linus.

News / Business

Announcing KataOS and Sparrow
KataOS is a new OS for embedded devices by Google. It is based on seL4, almost entirely in Rust and with a reference implementation of a secure system (built with OpenTitan on a RISC-V arch). It sounds great but, like I already wrote in the introduction: Can we safely invest in a Google product?

NuttX RTOS 11.0.0
This new major version of NuttX comes with hundreds of improvements and fixes. NuttX is primarily famous for me because it makes easy porting applications from Linux. I hope NuttX community won't be offended by my narrow point of view.

Tools / Libraries

Gobot
Gobot is a Golang framework for robotics, drones, and the IoT. For C developers, Go language is probably the closest language regarding the syntax. Be warned, it's a garbage-collected language.

Jobs

Linea Research, Embedded Software Engineer, Full-time, Letchworth Garden City, England, United Kingdom (Open to remote)
Linea Research Ltd, part of Focusrite Group PLC, designs and manufactures high performance audio electronics for the professional market. A global brand, their amplifiers and processors are used at some of the worlds most prestigious venues and events.

Misc

Reverse Engineering a Cat Feeder to Boost Productivity
A little fun side project by John Partee who creates his candy dispenser from a cat feeder. I'm not very convinced by using dog training techniques on myself to become a better developer, but at least it's fun!
item.author: embedsys

item.title: Stellar Quests 级别 1：管理优惠 | Stellar Quests Level 1: Manage Offers
item.description:
前任。 我想以 1,000 SALT 的价格出售 100 XLM。 这发生在 Stellar 的去中心化交易所（DEX）上。 从技术上讲，所有报价都是买入/卖出报价。 措辞上的不同是为了帮助其他人“推理”他们的提议。

要约执行的速度取决于去中心化交易所（DEX）上是否有相关的还价。 如果没有任何相关的反要约，则要约将一直有效，直到交易对手接受或删除为止。

管理购买优惠

购买报价是通过构建操作类型“管理购买报价”的交易来创建的。 此操作需要六个输入：销售、购买、购买数量、价格、报价 ID 和来源。 出售是您交换的资产。 购买是您获得的资产。 金额是您希望为此要约收到的购买资产的总金额。 价格是“一个单位买入的卖出价格”； 因此，如果我们以 1000 个 SALT 的价格出售 100 个 XLM，那么我们购买的每个 SALT 单位的成本为 0.10。 源帐户是创建报价的帐户的公钥； 此输入是可选的，因为如果报价不会由建立交易的帐户创建，那么此处输入的公钥将引用将创建报价的帐户。 注意：所有交易必须由提及的所有来源账户签署； 在这种情况下，建立交易的账户和实际创建的账户
报价。

管理销售报价

销售报价是通过构建操作类型“管理销售报价”的交易来创建的。 此操作需要六个输入：销售、购买、购买数量、价格、报价 ID 和来源。 出售是您交换的资产。 购买是您获得的资产。 金额是您希望为此要约收到的购买资产的总金额。 价格是“一个单位的卖出价格”； 因此，如果我们用 1000 SALT 购买 100 XLM，那么我们购买的每个 XLM 单位的成本为 10。Offer ID告诉交易做以下两件事之一：创建一个新的报价（例如 0），或更新一个现有的报价一个（例如 3948329）。 源帐户是创建报价的帐户的公钥； 此输入是可选的，因为如果报价不会由建立交易的帐户创建，那么此处输入的公钥将引用将创建报价的帐户。 注意：所有交易必须由提及的所有来源账户签署； 在这种情况下，建立交易的账户和实际创建的账户
报价。

被动销售

这是一个我仍在努力理解的概念，因为定义本身有很多矛盾 - 哈哈。 看一看...

被动出售要约创建了以一种资产出售另一种资产的要约，而无需采取等价的反向要约（??? 好的）。 这使您可以维护一个订单簿，该订单簿可以平等地买卖资产，而无需实际执行报价（???什么）。

奥特罗

我计划用相关的代码示例写相应的博客文章。 目前，我只是在学习 Stellar 区块链的基础知识并利用 Stellar 实验室与测试网进行交互。 如果您想了解 Stellar 区块链，并在做的过程中赚钱，请访问： https ://quest.stellar.org/

穆罕默德·阿亚安·西迪基 - 一旦暂停，gldnwzrd 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

原文:

An offer, on the Stellar blockchain, is when a party/person/thing decides to buy/sell one asset for another at a specific exchange rate; ex. I want to sell 100 XLM for 1,000 SALT. This takes place on Stellar's decentralized exchanged (DEX). There are three different types of offers on Stellar: Buy Offer, Sell Offer, and Passive Sell Offer. Technically, all offers are both buy/sell offers. The difference in wording is to help others "reason" about their offers.

How promptly an offer is executed depends on whether or not there's a relevant counter offer on the decentralized exchange (DEX). If there aren't any relevant counter offers, then the offer will sit until a counterparty takes it or until it's removed.

Manage Buy Offer

Buy offers are created by building a transaction of operation type "Manage Buy Offer". This operation asks for six inputs: selling, buying, buy amount, price, offer id, and source. Selling is the asset you're giving in exchange. Buying is the asset you're getting in exchange. The Amount is the required total of the buying asset you want to receive for this offer. The Price is the "price of one unit of buying in terms of selling"; so if we're selling 100 XLM for 1000 SALT, then each unit of SALT we're buying costs .10. Offer ID tells the transaction to do one of two things: create a new offer (Ex. 0), or update an existing one (Ex. 3948329). The Source Account is the public key of the account that'll be creating the offer; this input is optional because if the offer won't be created by the account which has built the transaction, then the public key entered here will refer to the account that will create the offer. NOTE: All transactions MUST be signed off by all source accounts mentioned; in this case, the account which built the transaction and the account which is actually creating
the offer.

Manage Sell Offer

Sell offers are created by building a transaction of operation type "Manage Sell Offer". This operation asks for six inputs: selling, buying, buy amount, price, offer id, and source. Selling is the asset you're giving in exchange. Buying is the asset you're getting in exchange. The Amount is the required total of the buying asset you want to receive for this offer. The Price is the "price of one unit of selling in terms of buying"; so if we're buying 100 XLM with 1000 SALT, then each unit of XLM we're buying costs 10. Offer ID tells the transaction to do one of two things: create a new offer (Ex. 0), or update an existing one (Ex. 3948329). The Source Account is the public key of the account that'll be creating the offer; this input is optional because if the offer won't be created by the account which has built the transaction, then the public key entered here will refer to the account that will create the offer. NOTE: All transactions MUST be signed off by all source accounts mentioned; in this case, the account which built the transaction and the account which is actually creating
the offer.

Passive Sell Offer

This is a concept I'm still struggling to understand because the definition itself has a lot of contradictions- lol. Take a look...

A Passive Sell Offer creates an offer to sell one asset for another without taking a reverse offer of equal price (??? Okay). This allows you to maintain an order book that both buys and sells an asset equally without the offers actually executing (??? What).

Outro

I plan on writing corresponding blog posts with relevant code examples. For now, I'm simply learning the basics of the Stellar blockchain and utilizing the Stellar Laboratory in order to interact with the test net. If you wish to learn about the Stellar blockchain, and earn while doing it, then please visit: https://quest.stellar.org/

Mohammad Ayaan Siddiqui - Oct 9

item.title: LeetCode 评估有意义吗？ | Do LeetCode Assessments Make Sense?
item.description:
查尔斯·克林顿·普斯特乔夫斯基三世

发表于几年来，我一直是一名专业的开发人员。 我从事过单体应用和微服务工作，调试过遗留代码并构建了新项目。 对于所有这些不同的项目，我的日常工作看起来都差不多：

  • 我与团队成员和利益相关者分解问题
  • 我使用我根据自己的喜好配置的 IDE 来解决这个问题
  • 当我遇到问题时，我会转向：
    • 我的团队成员
    • 谷歌
    • 堆栈溢出
    • 红迪网
    • 地鼠松弛
  • 我经常使用别人的代码作为模板或起点（即使只是我过去的代码）
  • 在适当的时候，我会导入 3rd 方库，这样我就不会重新发明轮子

作为后端开发人员，这些问题几乎总是与以下技术有关：

  • 服务器（REST、gRPC）
  • 数据库（SQL、MongoDB、DynamoDB）
  • 数据流（Kafka、AWS Kinesis、RabbitMQ）

所以当我面试一个后端开发人员职位时，为什么经常是技术评估：

  • 闭卷测试（没有谷歌等）
  • 基于数据结构和算法
  • 使用人为的问题
  • 使用我自己的文本编辑器或 IDE 以外的东西

当工作是关于数据库和服务器的时候，为什么公司会用数据结构和算法问题来评估我的技术技能？ 这似乎是错位的优先事项。 要明白我的意思，这里有一张图表，显示如果我们说 CPU 周期需要 1 秒并从那里缩放，各种事件将需要多长时间。

互联网调用比使用 CPU 和主内存的操作花费的时间要长得多。 鉴于此，为什么公司会关注我对“第二”和“分钟”操作的优化程度，而不是“数年”的操作？

这些问题的答案各不相同。 一些公司想效仿FAANG的榜样。 其他人没有给自己时间来创建和保持高质量的技术面试。

我决定不接受使用这些评估的面试。

这篇文章中的问题显示了 LeetCode 评估我的技能与我在工作中将要完成的技能之间的不匹配。

我更喜欢深入研究 Golang、SQL、DynamoDB、Kafka 和分布式系统的设计模式。 我磨练这些技能，因为它们将为与我合作的公司提供最大的价值。

这是有风险的。 由于这个决定，我拒绝了很多采访。 我降低了获得新工作的机会。 但我很少遇到喜欢这类评估或认为为他们学习可以很好地利用他们的时间的开发人员。 大多数人学习 LeetCode 是因为他们必须这样做，因为需要一份工作，而大多数工作都将其作为障碍。

作为一个行业，我们已经过去问自己为什么要使用这些技术评估。

如果你不同意我的观点，为什么你认为 LeetCode 风格的评估是有价值的、有用的等等？

如果您同意，有什么更好的选择？ 我的建议是遵循Laurie Barth的设计技术面试指南

技术世界与娜娜 - 一旦暂停，cpustejovsky 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，cpustejovsky 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发表，cpustejovsky 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 cpustejovsky 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 cpustejovsky 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 cpustejovsky 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Charles Clinton Pustejovsky III

Posted on Oct 21

I've been a professional developer for a few years now. I've worked on monoliths and micro-services, debugged legacy code and built greenfield projects. For all these different projects, my day-to-day has looked similar:

  • I break down the problem with team members and stakeholders
  • I work on the problem using my IDE of choice that I've configured to my liking
  • When I run into issues, I turn to:
    • My team members
    • Google
    • StackOverlow
    • Reddit
    • Gophers Slack
  • I often use other people's code as a template or a starting point (even if it's just my past code)
  • When appropriate, I import 3rd party libraries so I don't re-invent the wheel

As a back-end developer, these problems almost always relate to the following technologies:

  • Servers (REST, gRPC)
  • Databases (SQL, MongoDB, DynamoDB)
  • Data Streams (Kafka, AWS Kinesis, RabbitMQ)

So when I interview for a back-end developer position, why are technical assessments often:

  • closed book tests (no Google, etc.)
  • based on data structures and algorithms
  • using contrived problems
  • using something other than my own text editor or IDE

Why would companies assess my technical skills with data structure and algorithim questions when the job is about databases and severs? It seems like misplaced priorities. To see what I mean, here's a chart showing how long various events would take if we said a CPU cycle takes 1 second and scaled from there.

Internet calls take much, much longer than operations using the CPU and main memory. Given that, why would companies focus on how well I optimize for "second" and "minute" operations and not for the operations that take "years"?

The answers to these questions vary. Some companies want to follow FAANG's example. Others aren't giving themselves the time to create and maintain a quality technical interview.

I've decided that I won't entertain interviews that use these kinds of assessments.

The questions in this post show a mismatch between the skills LeetCode assesses me for and the skills I'd be doing at the job.

I prefer doing deeper dives into Golang, SQL, DynamoDB, Kafka, and design patterns for distributed systems. I hone those skills because those will provide the most value for companies I work with.

This is risky. I reject a lot interviews as a result of this decision. I decrease my chances of getting a new job. But I've rarely met developers who like these kinds of assessments or think studying for them is a good use of their time. Most people study LeetCode because they have to, because need a job and most jobs have this as an obstacle.

It's past time that we as an industry ask ourselves why we are using these kinds of technical assessments.

If you disagree with me, why do you think LeetCode style assessments are valuable, useful, etc.?

If you agree, what would be a better alternative? My suggestion would be to follow Laurie Barth's guide for Designing a Technical Interview

TechWorld with Nana - Sep 13

原文:

Small php logger is a simple php logger you can easily extends for your own need.

Basically, it implements possibility to log to : Standard output File (With log rotates)

Http service such as logstash

It it easy to implement your own Formatter or Output writer.

SwitchLogicInterface allow you to implements your own logic to manage multiple log streams.

It also possible to add shortcut to logger for easier use by final developer.

There is two composer packages :
sebk/small-logger which allow using for "from scratch" projects : https://github.com/sebk69/small-logger
sebk/small-logger-bundle is a symfony bridge for sebk/small-logger : https://github.com/sebk69/small-logger-bundle

item.title: 优化 Python 代码的 10 种方法 | 10 ways to optimize your Python code
item.description:
亚历克斯踏步

发表于使用 PEP 8
它包括有关如何格式化代码以使其更具可读性的建议。

  • 使用正确的数据结构
    选择正确的数据结构将帮助您优化代码。

  • 使用内置函数
    使用内置函数将帮助您优化代码。

  • 使用列表推导
    列表推导是 Python 的一个强大功能，它允许您从其他数据结构创建列表。 使用列表推导将帮助您优化代码。

  • 使用生成器
    生成器是 Python 的一个强大功能，它允许您创建迭代器。 使用生成器将帮助您优化代码。

  • 使用正确的库
    选择正确的库将帮助您优化代码。

  • 使用分析
    分析是一种强大的技术，可让您识别代码中的瓶颈。 使用分析将帮助您优化代码。

  • 使用缓存
    缓存是一种强大的技术，可让您将数据存储在内存中以便更快地访问。 使用缓存将帮助您优化代码。

  • 使用多处理
    多处理是一种强大的技术，它允许您使用多个处理器来运行您的代码。 使用多处理将帮助您优化代码。

  • 使用正确的工具
    选择正确的工具将帮助您优化代码。

    Alex Tread

    Posted on Oct 21

    1. Use PEP 8
      PEP 8 is the official style guide for Python code. It includes recommendations on how to format your code to make it more readable.

    2. Use the right data structures
      Python has a number of built-in data structures that you can use to store your data. Choosing the right data structure will help you optimize your code.

    3. Use the built-in functions
      Python has a number of built-in functions that you can use to manipulate data. Using the built-in functions will help you optimize your code.

    4. Use list comprehensions
      List comprehensions are a powerful feature of Python that allows you to create lists from other data structures. Using list comprehensions will help you optimize your code.

    5. Use generators
      Generators are a powerful feature of Python that allows you to create iterators. Using generators will help you optimize your code.

    6. Use the right libraries
      Python has a number of libraries that you can use to extend its functionality. Choosing the right library will help you optimize your code.

    7. Use profiling
      Profiling is a powerful technique that allows you to identify bottlenecks in your code. Using profiling will help you optimize your code.

    8. Use caching
      Caching is a powerful technique that allows you to store data in memory for faster access. Using caching will help you optimize your code.

    9. Use multiprocessing
      Multiprocessing is a powerful technique that allows you to use multiple processors to run your code. Using multiprocessing will help you optimize your code.

    10. Use the right tools
      Python has a number of tools that you can use to optimize your code. Choosing the right tool will help you optimize your code.

item.title: 如何使用Easy Escape Tarkov Cheats？ | How to use Easy Escape Tarkov Cheats?
item.description:
逃离塔科夫了解敌人的确切位置以及其他威胁可以帮助您避免过度使用弹药。 此外，游戏中充满了真实的建筑物，这些建筑物被摧毁、残骸和结构损坏，使得识别对手变得更加困难。 了解周围环境可以帮助您实现游戏目标，例如收集战利品并以安全的方式完成检查站。

对当前事态的敏锐意识对于逃离塔科夫秘籍的成功至关重要
确保成功玩《逃离塔科夫》的最重要方法之一是了解周围环境。 这是必不可少的，因为游戏没有 HUD。 这意味着玩家必须时刻注意周围的环境。 了解周围环境的能力对于确定正确的区域、收集战利品和安全地完成目标至关重要。
